I really don’t know. Now the question is, where would you stab him? In the wrist, the chest, the back, the elbow? Where? The backyard, the frontyard, the street, upstairs, downstairs, all around the town?
어디 보자, 당신은 ○○ 거리에 살고 있지요. 당신의 집 주소를 알고 있죠, 그렇지 않나요? 피닉스 시내에서 그 거리가 어디쯤인지도 알고 있고요? 당신의 집이 어느 방향을 향하고 있는지, 그리고 그 집에 누가 같이 살고 있는지도 알고 있죠? 어머니가 거기 함께 살고 계신데, 혹시 어머니가 다니시는 치과 의사의 이름을 알고 있나요?
So you can’t have an unreasoning fear about that dentist, can you?
And if you should want to go to that dentist, you know there are only seven days in the week. Would you prefer to go on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday? You can’t go on Sunday.

You could go in the forenoon; you could go in the afternoon. That is, on six forenoons you could go, or on six afternoons you could go. That is twelve possibilities of going—on Sunday, you can’t go. That is twelve possibilities of going, and only one possibility of not going.

Now, where would you be most likely to have that unreasoning fear of the dentist? It seems to me you really ought to have it when you are sitting in the dental chair. You certainly would not need to have it when you were leaving his office.
You don’t need to have a fear, an unreasoning fear of the dentist, when he is not going to work on your teeth.

Pregnancy is a fairly common thing. There is no real difficulty in achieving a pregnancy. You wouldn’t have an unreasoning fear of pregnancy if you knew you were pregnant. Le’t see, it would take you a whole month, at least, to find out that you were pregnant. You could be pregnant a whole month without an unreasoning fear. In fact, you could postpone the rabbit and frog tests for six weeks. That is, for six weeks you could be pregnant without any unreasoning fear. Now, that just tells me that you would not have an unreasoning fear for all of the pregnancy, but you do have an unreasoning fear. Just where is that unreasoning fear located? Fear that you might have twins? You know that you might. Triplets have happened too. This is your first baby, and I suspect you don’t want to double, triple, or quadruple your difficulities. You know you are going to handle that baby as if it’s made of very delicate china; you will be afraid it will come apart if you pick it up; you will try to bathe it with the gentlest of touches.
But certainly you will have an unreasoning fear, and I don’t know about what. In fact, I don’t know how long you are going to keep it.
It is going to make you rather thoughtful in selecting your obstetrician and, of course, you will choose a good hospital.
And, of course, in making the layette, or buying one, you see to it that it is a reasonably good layette.
Now, of course, you don’t know which month you are going to

As your pregnancy develops, I wonder what sort of an attitude you are going to take about the changes in your breasts? And I really wonder. What kind of an attitude are you going to take about the changes in your breasts? What does that mean? You are the kind of a person who takes an unreasoning attitude on some things. Are you going to take an unreasoning attitude about your breasts?
you certainly don’t want the calcium to be depleted, so you will see your dentist and you will watch your diet.

You know this question of a girdle, and just exactly what are you going to do about maternity clothes? About cutting down your athletic activities? You know that horseback riding—you are going to cut that out. You will miss it, but then you will comfort yourself after the delivery by returning to horseback riding.

This pain you had, this headache, is similar to the one you got after you bumped your head against the branch of a tree years ago. Do you mind closing your eyes, leaning back in your chair, and trying to describe the exact feelings you have in your head right now?
How cold is your hand?
That was only a slight dip in the plane right then, and now it seems to be smoothing out. Maybe another dip will occur in the plane, but it seems to be smoothing out.
You don’t have to have prior experience, but you can study it.

Say the worst possible word that you know connected with that subject, and just say that one word to me. And having said it, you won’t have to say a single other thing, only just the one word. It is the worst possible word, and then the next time you come in, you can feel that the worst has been said.
That is the worst possible word you can say to me. You really ought to be able to talk freely next time on a number of things.

During the intermission I got acquainted with you, didn’t I? Will you come up please? Now, as I recall it, you said you were a little bit afraid of hypnosis, and that you haven’t been in a trance before. You lack confidence in your husband’s ability.
That isn’t it at all.
Something else?
Do you think I ought to provide a more comfortable chair for you?
Let’s try the other chair.
How do you think I ought to go about putting you in a trance?
I will have to get you more relaxed than you are right now. Don’t you think it is nice to be tense once in a while?
Perhaps so, but suppose you tense up this hand. That is it. Now keep it tense even if it does get tired. Do you notice any tension in the other hand?
In your legs?

In your legs. All right. Let’s take the other hand. Keep that hand tense, and slowly relax this hand. And now this arm is comfortable, and your shoulders are comfortable, too. Are your shoulders comfortable?
I think your legs are getting more comfortable.
Do you want to make your right arm comfortable?
It is up to me. How much resistance is there now? It is all up to me. Relax this one. That is it. Now, what would be the best way of going into a trance?
You really don’t know, so I am going to suggest that you stay very, very wide awake—very, very wide awake, and you would like comfort about those contact lenses. Would you not?

All right. Now, speak about those contact lenses. Think about those contact lenses, and stay wide awake. Very, very wide awake, and think about those contact lenses. Now keep on thinking about the contact lenses, and keep right on thinking about the contact lenses. That is right, that is right. Now your eyes will be closed, and you can feel them under the eyelids, underneath. Just close your eyes and keep them closed.
Now that the end of the hour is approaching, you can open your eyes. And how do you feel?
What have you been doing?
That is right.
You’d swear that you haven’t, and do you ordinarily sit that quietly?
Where did the calmness come from?
“Maybe my heart was going pitter patter.” The calmness had to come from you, didn’t it? The rate of your heart beat...
And the quieting down. And by the way, how much attention did you pay to the audience?
Maybe you weren’t in a trance.
I was talking about her too, and when you talk about a woman and she doesn’t remember what you had to say—! So, you could develop an amnesia that quickly?
Maybe in this situation.
How did they look?
Look intelligent, alert? Have you ever before looked so appraisingly at a group of men?
Did you expect to be as calm, poised? You see, I am deliberately rocking you off balance again and again.

좋습니다. 이제 당신에게 질문을 하나 하려고 합니다. 나는 당신이 의식적으로 대답하는 것을 원치 않습니다. 당신도 알다시피, 아주 오래전에 당신은 '예'라고 할 때는 고개를 끄덕이고 '아니오'라고 할 때는 고개를 젓는 법을 배웠습니다. 그렇습니다. 이제 나는 당신의 무의식에게 질문을 던질 것입니다. 의식적으로 당신은 무의식이 내놓을 그 답을 알지 못합니다. 당신은 당신의 의식적인 답변만을 알고 있죠. 그것은 내가 원하는 것이 아닙니다. 그래서 나는 당신의 무의식에게 특정한 질문을 던질 것이고, 당신과 나는 당신의 무의식이 대답하기를 함께 기다릴 것입니다. 그러면 당신의 무의식은 고개를 끄덕이거나 가로저음으로써 대답할 것입니다. 이제 내가 당신의 무의식에게 던질 질문은 이것입니다. 당신은 트랜스 상태에 있었습니까?
No, that was your conscious mind that answered. Now, you wait. Were you in a trance? Slowly. That is right. What was the feeling?
That is right. You had the feeling. Isn’t that right? Now this time let your head nod slowly in answer to that question. Have you been in a trance? Slowly, let your head nod.
Is it very hot outside today?
It is usually hot outside in Phoenix in the summertime.

You are really enjoying that trance. I would like to have you people learn every conceivable thing of value to you. As a hypnotic subject, you are interested in contact lenses and you are interested in wearing them to a greater extent. You are not quite certain of the feeling in your eyeballs. You really don’t want to injure your eyeballs when you don’t know what feelings are really harmful; what feelings aren’t just a little bit different. Actually, of course, I think your unconscious mind will be able to make that analysis for you, and I think your unconscious mind can accustom you to the sensation of contact lenses. And I am very certain that your unconscious mind will detect any little change or something bad in the feel. You think that might be comfortable? All right. Now take it easily and gradually. I would like to have you feel exceedingly rested and comfortable and at ease and happy as you awaken. And continue, when you are awake, to have that feeling.
Hi. How do you feel?
We were talking about what I said to the audience. You remembered your arms.
What is it not as much this time?
You remembered your arm, not as much this time as you did last time.
By the way, how do you feel about wearing contact lenses?

What do you suppose you have under them? Maybe you are getting acquainted with the feeling. That is, if you had a numbness of your hand and couldn’t really feel adequately, completely with your hand, and then I were to touch you, you might feel it before you felt this. And you would feel this and this before you felt this. And you would feel this and this before you felt this; and not until you had located all parts of your hand would you feel complete touch. Do you understand? And I rather imagine that you have quite a bit to learn about the feeling of your eyeball under the contact lenses. It ought to be interesting, and I think you will just automatically learn to recognize ordinary new different feelings, and feelings that indicate adverse or wrong conditions; and your husband can explain that to you more fully. By the way, how does it feel to you when you forget the presence of the audience?
It does, and how much have you forgotten the presence of the audience? How many times?
You mean after looking over that handsome group you can forget them? But you can, can't you?
How do you think you do that?
You guess?
On what I was saying. And it is perfectly possible to forget them even with your eyes wide open. How is your right arm?
Had you forgotten that?
Yes. Was it much of a jar to get reconnected with it?
Because you gave quite a startled reaction as you reassociated your right arm. Now tell me, how did you learn to go into a trance?
But how do you know all the right ways to please me?
All right. Did you go into a trance without knowing you were going to go?
You don’t know how to answer that, and you really don’t, do you? Not at all. You really don’t, do you? That is right, and you really don’t. Just close your eyes.
And you really don’t like that obsessive fear, and you really don’t, and you really want to get rid of it; you’d really like to understand it.

And do you know, time is passing and you have been sitting here a whole hour? A long, long time. Long enough to feel awfully tired, and you sat here somewhere around 3:15, and then 3:30 came, and 3:45, 4:00; and you really ought to be home by 6:00. And it is getting pretty close to that time, and I don’t want you to worry too much about it. You won’t leave, but you really have to be home at 7:00, or somewhere else but not here, and it must be pretty close to 6:00 already. Now, I want you to wake up with the feeling you have been here too darned long.
Where do you think you will go now?
Why?
You’ve got to cook dinner?
Are you a good cook?
He looks well nourished. Besides, it is getting dark outside. How do you feel about me taking so long? It really wasn’t thoughtful, was it? Was it?
I had my purpose. What time do you cook, really?
Make a guess.
4:30 or 5:00. What makes you think it is 4:30 or 5:00?
You feel like you have been here a long time. Am I that boresome?
Haven’t you been learning a lot?
Well, why does it seem so long?

Do you know the opposite of time feeling long? The opposite is, time feeling short. You know time passes so rapdily that a whole afternoon can pass and it still feels as if it is the beginning of the afternoon. And that is such a nice feeling, isn’t it? It really does feel as if it is the beginning of the afternoon. About what time is it?
Seven minutes to four. All right. Now I want you to feel as if it were 2:30. We’ve done an awful lot of work in that time haven’t we?
Haven’t you?
You have washed the car. What else have you done?
What else?
And you got down here.
And you got down here. What time is it?
About six minutes to four.
What have I been talking about?
What does that mean? [E begins to move S’s hand.]
Got you on the spot?
Keep on talking. Explain about time.
All right. You can wake up now.
What am I talking about?
Didn’t it?
Why did you wake up?
How could you wake up if your unconscious hadn’t taken over?
I have you there. That is right.
How are your eyes?
They are fine. I think they are, too. They are delightful.

I would like to give you a posthypnotic suggestion. I am labeling it as a posthypnotic suggestion, not for you as the subject, but for them [as the audience] so that they will know I am giving you a posthypnotic suggestion. You know, there is a tomorrow coming, a next week, a next month, a next year. There are a lot of things of interest in the coming days, months, and years. Various things will happen. I want you to have every rightful pleasure and satisfaction; I want you to have every rightful freedom from distress. I would like to have you enjoy everything of value. Now close your eyes and think that over and apply it to yourself in all the ways that are needful, and when you have thought it over, just take a breath or two and wake up feeling rested and refreshed and energetic.
Hi!
It didn’t take us long to get on an informal basis, did it?
What is your first name?
Ingrid—of what descent?
That is too bad.
You know, 10,000 Swedes ran through the weeds pursued by one Norwegian!
I started that first. I think us squareheads should stick together. Don’t you?
이제, 당신이 최면에 들어갈 수 있다고 생각하나요? 만약 당신이 할 수 있다고 생각한다면, 당신의 오른손이 위로 올라갈 것입니다. 만약 당신이 최면에 들어갈 수 없다고 생각한다면, 당신의 왼손이 위로 올라갈 것입니다.

You have been demonstrating how to go into a trance and out of a trance; how to make time very, very long; how to gain poise and comfort; how to blot out the audience and pay no attention to them; how to go deeply into a trance; how to develop amnesia, anesthesia, and dissociation from the audience; how to develop poise and comfort. How do your eyes feel? You weren’t even thinking about them. You have learned a great deal. Do you suppose you could use hypnosis for anything else?
And you can use hypnosis with a great deal of comfort and satisfaction. All right. I am very much indebted to you for helping me out.

이제 당신의 의식적인 마음이 에테르의 향기 속으로 서서히 흩어지는 동안, 당신의 몸은 아주 오래전부터 간직해온 그 기억의 지도로 우리를 안내할 것입니다. 프로이트 박사와 함께했던 그 모든 논리적인 대화들은 잠시 잊어도 좋습니다. 지금 이 순간, 당신의 신경계는 세 살 무렵의 그 어린아이가 느꼈던 공기의 온도와 긴장감을 고스란히 재현하고 있습니다. 당신은 그저 그 아이의 눈이 되어, 그때 보았던 것을 지금 여기에서 다시 보고, 그 아이의 목소리가 되어 내게 들려주기만 하면 됩니다. 당신의 무의식은 이미 그곳에 도착해 있습니다. 무엇이 느껴지나요?

Following Clark L. Hull’s example, I made notes on a lot of observations on things that interested me and that I thought might some day be useful. A couple of weeks ago I happened to pull a folder out of my filing cabinet and found sheets of yellow and white paper bearing the enclosed material which I put together as a continuum. The first part was discussed with Larry Kubie and with Dave Rappaport when he was engrossed in writing his book on “Memory.”
It is all material that helped shape my thinking about hypnosis and the importance of factors seemingly totally unrelated.

One of my favorite recollections is an incident that occurred when I was standing in the barn doorway when I was about ten years old. A “brilliant” idea occurred to me, now long forgotten. I knew to execute that brilliant idea I would need a hammer and a hatchet. But those things were on the back porch. I rushed to get the tools, but in some way, by the time I reached the porch, I had completely forgotten what I was after. Following a long, fruitless mental search, I returned to the barn door and recalled my brilliant idea and what was needed to execute it. My brilliant idea was associated with the barn door where I happened to get it.

That led to climbing trees and learning poems from an old magazine of my grandmother’s. I picked trees at random, short poems at random, and noted the connection between individual trees and the poems I learned while sitting in those trees. Three years later I went on an exploration tour and found that when I climbed the right tree, my memory of the poem associated with it was greatly improved.

I didn’t want to try to struggle with her and tell her she should go into a trance, because that would be a rather futile thing. Therefore I asked her to do something that she could understand in her own reality orientations. I asked her to stay wide awake from the neck up. That was something she could understand. I told her to let her body go to sleep. In her past understandings as a child, as a youth, as a young woman, she had had the experience of a leg going to sleep, of an arm going to sleep. She had had the feeling of her body being asleep in that hypnagogic state of arousing in the morning when you are half awake, half asleep. I was very very certain the woman had some understanding of her body being asleep. Thus the woman could use her own past learnings. Just what that meant to her, I don’t know. All I wanted to do was to start a train of thinking and understanding that would allow the woman to call upon the past experiential learnings of her body.

The essential consideration in the use of ideomotor techniques lies not in their elaborateness or novelty but simply in the initiation of motor activity, either real or hallucinated, as a means affixing and focusing the subject’s attention upon inner experiential learnings and capacities.
